fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 67-year-old White male with schizophrenia presenting with severe deterioration.
fields.Collateral_Contact_Information: Emergency Contact: Adult Protective Services Worker (Linda Martinez) at 555-221-8834
fields.Subjective: Mr. Ferguson brought to emergency psychiatric evaluation by APS after neighbors reported concerns. Nursing home where he resides confirms he receives haloperidol 15 mg nightly via medication administration by staff with 100% documented adherence for 18 months. Despite perfect compliance, patient has catastrophically decompensated over past month. Appears severely psychotic, mumbling incoherently to unseen entities throughout interview. Staff report he experiences continuous command auditory hallucinations telling him to stop eating and drinking to 'purify his soul.' He has complied, refusing nearly all food and fluids for two weeks, resulting in 31-pound weight loss to 108 lbs, now severely malnourished and dehydrated. Also maintains fixed delusion that he is already dead and residing in purgatory. Spends days and nights screaming at hallucinations, disturbing other residents. Sleep absent for over week per staff. Personal hygiene severely neglected - refusing all bathing, smearing feces on walls. Violent outbursts when staff attempt to provide care or food. Completely unable to care for self. Staff frightened and requesting transfer. Mood difficult to assess given severity of psychosis. Risk assessment critical: Following command hallucinations to starve, life-threatening malnutrition and dehydration, gravely disabled. States he is already dead so cannot die. (Emergency psychiatric evaluation by Dr. Robert Harrison, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Elderly male, severely emaciated, disheveled, malodorous, actively psychotic
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Severe command auditory hallucinations, nihilistic delusions

Behavioral_Health_Checkup.C_SSRS: Severe risk - following command hallucinations to starve, gravely disabled

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Critical labs: BUN 58 mg/dL, Cr 2.3 mg/dL acute renal failure; sodium 151 mEq/L severe hypernatremia; albumin 2.1 g/dL severe malnutrition; glucose 62 mg/dL.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizophrenia with acute severe exacerbation, treatment-refractory, grave disability with life-threatening complications.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Resides in nursing home; never married; no family; isolated; on Medicare/Medicaid; multiple prior psychiatric hospitalizations.
Assessment_and_Plan: Perfect medication adherence documented through facility administration.
Complete treatment failure with life-threatening deterioration.
Immediate medical danger from malnutrition, dehydration, and renal failure.
Requires emergency medical hospitalization for stabilization before psychiatric transfer.
Will need IV hydration, nutritional support, possibly NG tube.
After medical stabilization, psychiatric admission for clozapine trial and possibly ECT.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Robert Harrison, MD, Psychiatrist — County Crisis Services, 7821 Emergency Boulevard, Sacramento, CA 95814, Phone: (555) 776-2398, Fax: (555) 776-2399
